ety: khiyā˚ = S. क्षय+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.n. To be worn, to be abraded; — v.t. To rub away, abrade; to rub down.

ety: khiyā˚ = khiyāw˚ = Prk. खयाव(इ) = S. खाद (contrac. to खद, rt. खाद्)+अपय(ति)
def: v.t. (dialec.) caus. of khānā, and = khilānā, q.v.

ety: for A. خيانة, inf. n. of خان, for خون 'to be unfaithful, or perfidious,' &c.
def: s.f. Unfaithfulness (to one's confidence, or to a trust); perfidy, treachery, treason; breach of trust, misappropriation; embezzlement; dishonesty, knavery
subs: (-meṅ), To be unfaithful to a trust; to be guilty of breach of trust, to misappropriate, to embezzle; to practise perfidy or treachery.

ety: subst. fr. iḵẖtiyār, inf. n. of viii of خير 'to be good,' &c.
def: s.m. Election, choice, option.

ety: the A. being der. fr. P. ḵẖiyār = H. ḵẖīrā, fr. S. क्षीर
def: s.m. A cucumber, Cucumis sativus
adj. (Dakh.) Sly, artful, deceitful.
s.m. (obl. dual of ḵẖiyār, q.v.), The two kinds of cucumbers, i.e. the cucumber and the musk-melon; the seeds of the cucumber and musk-melon (used medicinally).
s.f. Scurfiness; scurf, dandriff.

ety: khiyā˚ = khiyāw˚ = Prk. खयाव(इ) = S. खाद (contrac. to खद, rt. खाद्)+अपय(ति)
def: v.t. (dialec.) caus. of khānā, and = khilānā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of خضب 'to colour or tinge'
def: s.m. Tinging or dyeing the nails and hair (especially the beard), hair-dye, hair-tincture (usually of a red or dark blue colour)
subs: To apply a razor (to the chin, &c.), to shave (the beard)
(-meṅ), To apply hair-dye, to dye the hair black.

def: s.f. Autumn, the fall of the leaf; — decay, old age
subs: adj. Having seen or experienced autumn.

ety: for A. خزانة ḵẖizānat, fem.; v.n. fr. خزن 'to stow, to guard,' &c.
def: s.m. A repository, magazine, store-room, granary; a treasury; — treasure, revenue, finances; — chamber (of a gun)
subs: adj. & s.m. Having a chamber (a gun); — a gun or musket with a chamber
s.m. The royal treasury, the Government treasury

def: adj. Autumnal; fading.
prop. n.; see ḵẖẉāja ḵẖiẓr, s.v. ḵẖẉāja.
s.m. = khoʼā or khoyā, q.v.
s.f. Nature, disposition, temper; habit, custom; way, manner
subs: v.n. A habit to be acquired; to become a habit
(-kī), To naturalize; to habituate, accustom, use (to), inure; to train; to exercise; to adopt a habit; to introduce a custom or practice
adj. Rendered well-behaved, or tractable, or mild; tamed; — accustomed, habituated.

def: s.m. Pounded brick, coarse brickdust (syn. surkhī); mortar, plaster; — milk inspissated by boiling, curds.
part. & s.m. = khoyā, q.v.
s.m. A hog-sty.
s.f. = khoṅć, q.v.
s.m. = khoṅćā, q.v.
s.m. = khoṅćaṛ, q.v.

ety: Zend khaodha, fr. khud; S. kuha
def: s.m. A helmet, steel head-piece.
ex: میں یہ کام خود کروں گا۔
I will do this work myself.

def: s.f. Digging; dig; — push, thrust, pass, stab
subs: s.f. Strict or close search; searching inquiry.

def: s.f. prop. khudāʼī, q.v.
v.t. prop. khudānā, q.v.
adj. Pitted; rough, uneven, &c. ( = khudrā); — s.m. A kind of pace (in a horse), a trot, an amble (see khudrānā).

ety: S. कोटर; or khoḍa = khoda (see khodnā)+S. रः
def: s.m. A pit, cavity, hole, hollow (in a tree, &c.; cf. kholaṛ).
adj. = khudrā, q.v.
adj. Sixteenth.
s.f.(?) (Dakh.) = khodnī, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) Digging, delving; culture (of land).

r: khod-kar, khod-ke, khod-khod-ke, khodnā, khod-kar pūćhnā, ko
def: = H کهودکے खोदके khod-ke = H کهودکے کهود खोदखोदके khod-khod-ke (past conj. part. of khodnā), By dint of digging; by close application or search; searchingly, closely
subs: (-ko), To question closely or searchingly; to investigate minutely; to cross-question; to probe.

r: khod-ke, khod-kar, khod-khod-ke, khodnā, khod-kar pūćhnā, ko
def: = H کهودراکر खोदकर khod-kar = H کهودکے کهود खोदखोदके khod-khod-ke (past conj. part. of khodnā), By dint of digging; by close application or search; searchingly, closely
subs: (-ko), To question closely or searchingly; to investigate minutely; to cross-question; to probe.

r: khod-khod-ke, khod-kar, khod-ke, khodnā, khod-kar pūćhnā, ko
def: = H کهودراکر खोदकर khod-kar = H کهودکے खोदके khod-ke (past conj. part. of khodnā), By dint of digging; by close application or search; searchingly, closely
subs: (-ko), To question closely or searchingly; to investigate minutely; to cross-question; to probe.

ety: khod˚, prob. = Prk. खोद(इ) or खोदे(इ) = S. क्षोदय(ति), caus. of rt. क्षुद्
def: v.t. To dig, delve, excavate; to undermine; to scoop, hollow; to engrave; to carve; — to search for; to inquire closely into, to investigate
subs: v.t. (intens.) To dig up; to scoop out, &c.

def: s.f. Digging, &c.; — searching; investigating; — instrument for digging; a spade; engraving-tool; carving-tool (cf. khudnī)
subs: To search for by digging; to dig for treasure; to search for, investigate; to find out.

def: v.t. prop. khudwānā, q.v.
adj. See s.v. ḵẖo.
s.m. The stuffing of a saddle; a pad; a saddle-cloth; a pack-saddle
subs: s.f. Padding or stuffing of a saddle; rags, rubbish, trash.

def: adj. See s.v. ḵẖo.
s.m.f. = kho, 'a cave,' &c., q.v.
s.f. A hood-form covering of leaves, &c. to shade plantlets, &c.); a kind of hood (worn as a protection against rain); clothes folded up and put on the head (as a defence against rain, &c.).
s.f. Swollen parched grain (esp. rice; syn. khīl, lāwā).

ety: fem. of khoʼā or khoyā; and = S. क्षपिता
def: s.f. Refuse, dregs; husk; the refuse of sugar-cane after the juice has been expressed.

ety: fem. of khoʼā or khoyā; and = S. क्षपिता
def: s.f. Refuse, dregs; husk; the refuse of sugar-cane after the juice has been expressed.
s.m. Search, inquiry, quest; research, strenuous effort; — mark, trace, track, clue (syn. talāsh, patā, surāg̠)
subs: s.f. Inquiry, investigation, close or strict search; strenuous effort; vexation
(-kā), To lose all trace (of); — to wipe out all trace (of); to ruin, mar
(-kā), To search (for); to trace, track, follow up a clue
khoj malyā-meṭ karnā (-kā), To wipe out all trace (of); to efface the footprints (of)
To obtain (or find) the track (of, -kā); to get an inkling (of)
(-ke), To be on the look out for faults (on the part of), to endeavour to pick a hole (in another's coat)
(-kā), To discover the track (of), or a clue (to); to trace (stolen property).

def: s.m. A eunuch (i.e. one who has been castrated).
s.m. A eunuch (i.e. one who has been castrated).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā) = khojī, q.v.
v.t. To cause to search or seek for; to cause to inquire, &c.
s.m. = khoj, q.v.

ety: khoj, q.v. + Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: s.m. Searching, seeking, inquiring; — inquisitive person; — tracker, detective (syn. surāg̠ī).

ety: khoj, q.v. + Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. Searching, seeking, inquiring; — inquisitive person; — tracker, detective (syn. surāg̠ī).

ety: prob. Prk. खुज्ज(इ) = S. क्षुद्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. क्षुद्
def: v.t. To search for, seek, inquire after; to explore
subs: khojani-hār, or khojan-hār, s.m. Searcher, seeker, inquirer, investigator.

ety: khoj + ṛā = Prk. अडओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. (colloq.) Mark, sign, trace ( = khoj, and gen. used in contempt).
adj. & s.m. = khokhā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. A dialec. form of khokhal, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.), A boy, a little child (cf. ćhokṛā).

ety: prob. fr. S. क्षप्, caus. of rt. क्षि; cf. also S. शुष्कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Decayed; hollow, &c. ( = khokhlā); — a discharged hunḍī or draft, a paid bill of exchange (kept as a voucher).

ety: khokha, q.v.+Prk. अडो and अडओ = S. रः and र+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Decayed, worn out; worm-eaten; hollow, excavated, empty (as a tree, or tooth, &c.); broken, cracked (as an earthen vessel); — s.m. A hollow tree; a decayed or hollow tooth, &c.
1˚ corr. of kokil; — 2˚ corr. of kokhā or kokh; — 3˚ = khokhā, 'boy.'
s.f. (dialec.) A girl, a little girl (cf. ćhokṛī).

ety: khokha, q.v.+Prk. अडो and अडओ = S. रः and र+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Decayed, worn out; worm-eaten; hollow, excavated, empty (as a tree, or tooth, &c.); broken, cracked (as an earthen vessel); — s.m. A hollow tree; a decayed or hollow tooth, &c.
adj. = khokhlā, q.v.
adj. = khokhlā, q.v.; — s.m. A kind of net; — the hollow or dead sound given out by a cracked or broken vessel when tapped.
s.m. A cover, case, sheath; a hollow.
ex: to apply a covering; to cover
wety: A hyperforeignism of کھول (khol), which is inherited from Sanskrit खोलि (kholi, “quiver”).

def: s.f. The act of opening, &c.; — s.m. A cavity, hollow (as of a tree, &c.); hull; hold (of a boat or ship); — a case, sheath, scabbard
s.f. Opening, unloosing, &c. (see kholnā).

ety: khol, q.v.+Prk. अडो = S. र +कः; but cf. khoḍar
def: s.m. Cavity, hollow (as of a tree, &c.).

ety: khol, q.v.+Prk. अडो = S. र +कः; but cf. khoḍar
def: s.m. Cavity, hollow (as of a tree, &c.).
adv. Openly, plainly, out, without reservation

ety: khol˚ = Prk. खोड(इ), or खोल(इ) = S. खोडय(ति), rt. खुड्
def: v.t. To open, loosen, loose, let loose, set free, let out; to unfasten, untie, unlock; to unmoor (a boat, &c.); to disentangle, unravel; to solve; to open out, unfold, display; to explain; to uncover, lay bare; to disclose, reveal; — v. int. To expand (itself); to burst forth; to shine, &c. (see khulnā)
subs: and kholan-hār, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Opening; (in Med.) deobstruent; detergent; — opener, looser, untier; solver, &c.

ety: khol, q.v.+Prk. अडो = S. र +कः; but cf. khoḍar
def: s.m. Cavity, hollow (as of a tree, &c.).

ety: kho˚ = Prk. खिव(इ) = S. क्षिप(ति), rt. क्षिप्
def: v.t. To cause to be lost or destroyed; to lose; to fail of; to part with, get rid of; to do (or make) away with, to throw away, to waste, squander; — s.m. Losing, &c.
subs: (intens.) To lose, to sacrifice; to make away with, to squander
or kho-ḍālnā, v.t. (intens.) To lose; to destroy; to get rid of, &c.
(intens.) To scatter to the winds, to squander, waste, dissipate, make away with.

def: s.f. Scratch, rent (in cloth, &c.), rip, puncture, stab
subs: or khoṅć lagnā (-ko, or -meṅ), To receive a scratch, or rip, &c.; to be ripped, to be torn (by a nail, &c.).

def: s.m. Thrust; stab, prick; stuffing
subs: s.f. Thrusting, stuffing; — mutual tugging and pulling, mutual wrangling and quarrelling, a scrimmage.

ety: fr. S. षष्, after the analogy of ḍhauṅćā, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Six and a half; — the multiplication table of six and a half times.
s.m. One who thrusts himself where he is not wanted; an obtrusive, officious fellow; a mean fellow.

s.f. Anything stuffed or inserted into another (as grass into thatch); stuffing, padding; — trifling purchases; — a handful (or small portion) of grain (out of that which is to be parched) taken as payment by the grain-parcher; — a trifle paid by those who draw water at a well to the bihishtī who attends there with his bucket, for the use of it.
v.t. To scratch, tear, rip open; to thrust, stuff (into); to prick, puncture, pierce, stab.
s.m. = khoṅdhā, q.v.
s.m. Gleanings or leavings on the threshing-floor (after the grain has been removed).
s.m. A (bird's) nest.
s.m. A cavity, hole, hollow (in a tree, &c.).
adj. (f. -ī), Hollow, decayed; — toothless.
s.f. Coughing; a cough; — asthma.
v.n. To cough; to hem, hawk.
s.f. = khop, q.v.
s.m. 1˚ = khopā, q.v.; — 2˚ = khop, q.v.
s.m. The hair (of the head) braided or tied up on the top of the head, hair-knot, top-knot (syn. jūṛā).
s.f. = khopri, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) = khosā, q.v.
v.t. = khosnā, q.v.
s.m. = khoṭ, q.v.
adj. = khoṭā, q.v.

ety: i.q. khoṅdhā = kho or khop, q.v. +S. धा
def: s.m. A (bird's) nest.
s.f. = khoṭāʼī = khuṭāʼī, q.v.

ety: i.q. khoṅdhā = kho or khop, q.v. +S. धा
def: s.m. A (bird's) nest.
s.m.f. Wasteful, extravagant, prodigal; — a spendthrift, a squanderer.
s.f. A hole, cave; a corner, a chasm, gap, fissure, rent; — a long stitch
subs: (-kī), To sew with long stitches, to baste.

def: s.m. = khoṅpā, q.v.
s.m. Cocoa-nut kernel, milk of a cocoa-nut hardened into a ball; — skull; brain (in this sense khoprā).
s.f. The skull, pate, sconce; the brain-pan; a shell
subs: (-kī), 'To devour (one's) skull or brains'; to eat (one's) head off; to torment, worry, plague; to spend or lavish the property (of another)
(-kī), To beat (one's) skull bare or bald; to beat or cudgel severely
(-kī), To make a hole in the skull (of); to trepan.

def: s.f. The skull, pate, sconce; the brain-pan; a shell
subs: (-kī), 'To devour (one's) skull or brains'; to eat (one's) head off; to torment, worry, plague; to spend or lavish the property (of another)
(-kī), To beat (one's) skull bare or bald; to beat or cudgel severely
(-kī), To make a hole in the skull (of); to trepan.

def: s.m. Cocoa-nut kernel, milk of a cocoa-nut hardened into a ball; — skull; brain (in this sense khoprā).
s.f. = khoṛ, q.v.

ety: S. कुहरं; but cf. khol, and khoḍar
def: s.f. A cavern, cave, &c. ( = khorā, khoh); a small valley; an alley, a lane; — a covering, cover; case ( = khol); a fine skin over the viscera; — the guts of cows or sheep (well cleaned and dressed, given to lying-in women to restore their milk when deficient or lost).
s.f. The wrath or curse of a god or spirit; — an unlucky moment (cf. khoṭ); disease; — an earthen trough (for cattle to eat out of; — cf. khoḍar, and khol).
s.m.f. A dialec. form of khoṭ, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) A piece (of wood, esp. of sandal-wood, or of perfume); a block, a log.
adj. (f. -ī), Limping, lame.
s.m. Stocks (for criminals); handcuffs, manacles; — a log of wood (used to encumber an animal whilst grazing), a clog, fetter.
s.m. A pit; cavern, cave, den ( = kho).
s.m. = khoḍar, q.v.; — adj. & s.m. = khodarā, or khudrā, q.v.
s.f. = khor, and khorā, qq.v.
s.f. = khauṛ, q.v.

ety: dim. cf khor or khorā, qq.v.
def: s.f. A narrow valley; — an alley; — a covering; cover.
s.f. Wickedness, vice, corruptness, &c.
adj. & s.m. corr. of koṛhī, q.v.

ety: khoṛ = khoṛ + Prk. इल्लो = S. इलः
def: adj. (Dakh.) Worthy of blame, disgraceful, &c. (see khoṭā).
s.m. Stock or stump (of a tree); a tree of which the head and branches are broken off; a dry or withered tree.
s.m. (dialec.) Rind, peel, skin, shell; — name of a marauding tribe on the borders of Sind.
adj. Having little or no beard (a man).
s.m. An ear of corn; a spike; a bunch, cluster (of grapes, or dates, &c.; syn. guććhā); — an ear-ring
subs: s.m. A gleaner; (fig.) recipient of a benefaction

def: v.t. To pull out (hairs, &c.); to pluck; to take or snatch away, to dart at and carry off; — to force (into, -meṅ), thrust (into), stick; to tuck (in); to insert; to stuff, cram ( = khoṅsnā).

s.f. & m. Falsehood, deceit, fraud; unfaithfulness, insincerity; vice, evil, wickedness; evil propensity or disposition; fault, defect, blemish, flaw; blunder, mistake, false step; — adulteration, alloy; — loss (in trade or dealing); injury, hurt, harm, mischief
subs: To make a mistake, to blunder; to commit a fault; to practise fraud or deceit, &c.; — to do harm (to, -kā or -kī), to injure, cause loss (to)
(-meṅ), To mix base metal (with), to adulterate
(-meṅ), To detect faults (in); to find fault (with).

def: s.m. = khoṅtā, q.v.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), False, base, counterfeit, spurious, forged; debased, alloyed, adulterated; faulty, defective, deficient; — false, unreal, untrue, insincere; perfidious, treacherous, deceitful, dishonest, fraudulent; base, corrupt; vicious, immoral, wicked, undutiful evil, bad, sinister; malignant, malicious, spiteful; — a false or perfidious man, a traitor; a wicked person, &c.
subs: To distinguish (or separate) good from bad
or khoṭī kharī sunānā (-ko), To abuse, to use bad language to
(-meṅ?), to alloy, adulterate; to vitiate, mar, spoil; to act falsely (to), play (one) false; to do harm or mischief (to)
To speak ill (of), speak against, backbite, abuse.

ety: khoṭā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: Baseness (of coin), adulteration, alloy; flaw; corruption; deceit, fraud, falseness, &c. &c. (see khoṭā, and khuṭāʼī).

ety: khoṭā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: Baseness (of coin), adulteration, alloy; flaw; corruption; deceit, fraud, falseness, &c. &c. (see khoṭā, and khuṭāʼī).
s.m. = khoṭā-pan, q.v.
s.m.f. Wasteful, extravagant, prodigal; — a spendthrift, a squanderer.

ety: for khoʼā, perf. part. of khonā; and = S. क्षपित+कः
def: part. adj. & s.m. (f. khoʼī), Lost; destroyed; thrown away, &c.; — any thing or person that is lost; that which is thrown away; refuse, dregs.
s.m. = khoʼā, q.v.
s.m. Christ.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of or relating to Christ or to His religion, Christian; — a Christian.

ety: Pehl. ḵẖvap; Zend hvāpāo, hu+āpa; S. su+apas
def: adj. & adv. Splendid, beautiful, pretty, charming; good; fine; excellent; pleasing, amiable; — very good; very well; well, excellently, &c.
subs: (compar. of ḵẖūb), adj. More beautiful; finer; better
(superl.), adj. Most beautiful, fairest; best, finest
ḵẖūb-ṣūrat, adj. & s.f. Having a beautiful face, beautiful, handsome; — a beauty
ḵẖūb-ṣūratī, s.f. Comeliness of face or form, beauty.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خُوب (xūb). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi خوب (xvb /⁠xūb⁠/).

def: s.m.f. pl. (of ḵẖūb), The fair, the beautiful; (as a sing.) Sweetheart, lady-love.
s.f. An apricot; a dried apricot (with its kernel, or that of an almond inserted).
v.t. = khubnā, q.v.
s.f. Beauty; goodness, excellence; merit, virtue; well-being; pleasantness
s.f. Name of a seed of a cooling quality.

ety: khub˚ = khubh˚ = Prk. खुब्भे(इ) = S. क्षोभय(ति), rt. क्षुभ्
def: v.t. To stir, to affect; to enter, penetrate, prick, pierce, stick into (cf. ćubhnā); — to adorn.
s.m. = ḵẖabās̤at, q.v.
adj. See s.v. ḵẖūb.
s.m. = ḵẖawīd, q.v.
ex: میں یہ کام خود کروں گا۔
I will do this work myself.

def: s.f. Dregs, refuse.
s.f. corr. of خويد ḵẖawīd, q.v.

ety: old P. qadhāi, qudhāi; Pehl. khotāi; Zend qadhāta, qa+dāta, rt. dā = S. sva+dhita, rt. dhā
def: adj. God-worshipping, godly, devout
subs: s.f. The worship of God; devoutness
adj. God-fearing; virtuous, upright; — compassionate, merciful
s.f. The quality of fearing God; piety; goodness, virtue, uprightness

God (be your) protector! God keep you! adieu! farewell! — ḵẖudā-ḵẖudā-kar-ke, adv. After earnest and repeated imploring of the aid of God, after much prayer; with great difficulty; by hook or by crook
adj. Given of God, granted by God; (hence) natural; — a proper name
(-ko), lit. 'God to be manifest'; to think of God in danger or trouble
God will avenge! — ḵẖudā-se kām paṛnā, To cast (one's) burden on God; to have recourse to God for aid (in a matter)
adj. & s.m. Knowing God; — one who knows God; — ḵẖudā-shināsī, s.f. Knowledge of God
The abode of God, heaven; the house of God, a place of worship; a mosque; — ḵẖudā-ke ghar se phirnā, To have a narrow escape from death; — to recover from a dangerous illness
To act justly, be just, do justice
God grant; would to God! — ḵẖudā-kī panāh, (I seek) the protection of God; God preserve (me, &c.)
ḵẖudā-kī mār, s.f. Divine vengeance or wrath
To be stricken, or to suffer, by the decrees of fate; to be unfortunate
To speak as before God, to speak the truth, say what is right
or ḵẖudā na-kare, or ḵẖudā na-ḵẖẉāsta or na-ḵẖẉāsta bāshad, God forbid. lest ( = mubādā)
ex: مَخْلُوق میں خالِق نَظَر آیا ہو جِس کو اُس دیکھْنے والے نے خُدا کو دیکھا ہَے۔
maxlūq mẽ xāliq nazar āyā ho jis ko us dekhne vāle ne xudā ko dekhā hai.
Who has seen the creator in the creation, that observer has seen God.

def: adj. Divine; — s.f. God. ship, godhead, divinity, providence; almighty power, omnipotence; — creation, nature, the world
subs: s.f. Singing hymns and watching all night, a vigil, a religious feast kept by women at night
s.m. A kind of food given to the poor in completion of a vow
s.m. A world-famed or notorious liar
To perform something miraculous; to show or use divine power
(kisī-ke), To be puffed up with pride (from the accession of fortune).

def: s.f. Digging, excavating; engraving; carving.

ety: caus. of khodnā, q.v.; — khudā˚, prob. = Prk. खुडावे˚ = S. खण्ठ+आपय˚, rt. खण्ड्
def: v.t. To cause to dig or excavate; to cause to engrave; to cause to carve; — to cause to be dug, &c. ( = khudwānā).
s.f. (rare) A goddess.

ety: see ḵẖudā, q.v.+wand = Zend vañṭ = S. वन्त्
def: s.m. Owner, possessor, master, lord; husband; — my lord! your lordship! Sir! — ḵẖudāwand-ě-něʻmat (a form of address to superiors), Lord of bounty, beneficent master; — adj. Possessing wealth, rich, opulent.
s.f. Ownership, lordship, sovereignty; divinity, providence; the property or quality of a beneficent lord or master, favour.
s.f. = khudāʼī, q.v.
(voc. of ḵẖudā), O God.
s.m. A great lord, a sovereign, a great king.
s.m. pl. (of ḵẖādim), Domestics, servants, ministers.
s.f. = khudḍī, q.v.

ety: prob. Prk. खुडिआ; S. खण्ड+इका; cf. the form khudde (khuddī?); cf. also khudnā and khodnā
def: s.f. A small pit or hollow; a space, interstice, opening, gap, chink; the hole and apparatus made in a horse's stall to receive his urine; one of the separate brick spaces or compartments in a necessary; the space from which a tooth has fallen out or been extracted, a hollow or gap between two teeth; — an oblong patch (from the forehead to the crown of the head) from which the hair has been shaven.
s.f. Hunger; appetite (syn. bhūk)
subs: (-ko), To feel hunger, to be hungry
(˚dhā+ār˚), adj. & s.m. Distressed with hunger, hungry, famished; — a very hungry person.

def: v.n. To be broken up (ground), to be dug, be delved, be excavated, be hollowed; to be engraved; to be carved
s.f. Digging; mining; search (by digging) for treasure, &c.; — digging-instrument, a spade
subs: (-kī), To dig (for treasure), to search (for treasure) by digging.

def: adj. Small, little, petty, &c. (see kshudra).
s.f. = kshudrā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Having small holes or hollows, pitted; rough, rugged; jagged, notched; — pock-marked; — (local) miscellaneous.
v.n. (dialec.) To trot; to amble.
v.n. corr. of کهدرانا khudrānā, q.v. and cf. khodar).
s.f. Digging; engraving; carving; — price or pay for digging; cost of, or charge for, engraving, &c.
v.t. To cause to be dug or excavated, to have or get dug; to cause to be engraved, or carved; to have (a plate, &c.) engraved, &c.
s.f. corr. of khudhā, q.v.
s.m. A boot.

ety: v.n. of ḵẖuftan, Pehl. ḵẖwaftan, qaftan; Zend qap; S. svap
def: s.f. Sleeping; sleep.

ety: = S. सुप्त, rt. स्वप्; see ḵẖuft
def: part. adj. Sleeping, asleep; put to sleep.

ety: for A. خفية ḵẖufyat, fem.; inf. n. of خفي 'to hide oneself,' &c.
def: adj. & adv. Concealed, hidden, disguised; secret, private; — covertly, secretly, privately, stealthily, clandestinely
subs: To carry on a business or work secretly or privately; to investigate secretly or privately, make private inquiry
s.m. A secret correspondent, an informer
s.f. The writing of secret intelligence; secret intelligence; the business or office of a secret intelligencer.

ety: for A. خفيةً, acc. of ḵẖufyat
def: adv. Secretly, privately.
v.n. To itch, to feel itchy.

ety: khuj˚, prob. = S. खर्जू+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā
def: v.t. To scratch (with the nails); to titillate, tickle; — v.n. To itch, to feel itchy.

ety: ḵẖū = Zend hu = S. su;+jista = Zend ćista, rt.ćit = S. ćit
def: adj. Happy, fortunate, auspicious, blest.
s.m. Sediment, dregs, refuse; mouldiness (of vinegar, &c.).

ety: prob. khujhrā = khujrā = khujlā, rt. of khujlānā, q.v.+āhā = S. अक+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Stingy, niggardly, miserly, sordid; — a niggard, miser (syn. kr̤ipaṇ; ka njūs; Shakespear's Dictionary has 'stringy' instead of 'stingy, — a typographical error, no doubt; Forles copies 'stringy,' and adds 'ropy,' in which he is followed by Bate!).

r: khujlāhaṭ, khujlā, nā, āhat, āwaṭ, khijāwaṭ
def: s.f. Scratching; itching, itchiness; titillation.
v.t. & v.n. = khujānā, q.v.
s.m. = khujlāhaṭ, q.v.

ety: Prk. खज्जुडिआ or खज्जुलिआ; S. खर्जु+ल+इका
def: s.f. Itchiness; the itch; the mange (in dogs)
subs: or khujlī honā, v.n. The itch to break out; to have the itch.

def: adj. & s.m. = khujhrāhā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to be scratched, to get or have scratched; — v.t. &n. = khujlānā, q.v.

ety: Pehl. khūk; Zend hu; S. sūkara
def: s.m. A boar, hog, swine, pig.
adj. Hollow, empty ( = khukhlā, or khokhlā); destitute poor, penniless, miserable.
s.f. A small insect that appears in wheat and barley after heavy rain and produces a red blight.
adj. (f. -ī), Hollow, &c. ( = khokhlā, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ī), Hollow, &c. ( = khokhlā, q.v.).
s.m. Divorcement of a wife for a ransom given by her, or for a gift or compensation from her or from another.

part. adj. (f. ī), Open, unclosed, not shut up; uncovered; exposed, bare, naked; not tied up, not fastened, unconfined; loose; free, unrestricted, unrestrained; clear, unclouded, fair (as the sky); — wide, broad, expanded; plain, clear, evident, manifest, undisguised, overt; public, known
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Open, public, well-known; plain, fair, straightforward; wide apart; clear, distinct (as writing, &c.)
part. adj. (f. -ī), Open, &c. ( = khulā); (in Bot.) polyphyllous
adv. In the open market; openly, publicly; fearlessly; — expressly; manifestly
adv. With loose or dishevelled hair
adv. Unfettered, unrestrained, at large; — freely; openly
adv. 'In the open treasury'; openly, &c. ( = khule-bāzār)
and khullam-khullā, adv. Openly, &c. ( = khule-bāzār, q.v.)
s.m. A house in an open and airy situation
adv. In the open plain; openly, &c. ( = khule-bāzār).

def: s.m. pl. (of ḵẖalīfa), Successors; princes; the Caliphs.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خلفا (xolafâ), from Arabic خُلَفَاء (ḵulafāʔ).

def: s.f. Causing to open; opening.

ety: for A. خلالة, v.n. fr. خلّ 'to pierce'
def: s.f. What comes forth from the teeth when they are picked; bits of food sticking between the teeth.
v.t. To cause to open, &c.; — to open, &c.
(in A. ḵẖalanjān), s.m. prop. خولنجان ḵẖūlinjān, q.v.

ety: for A. خلاصة ḵẖulāṣat, fem.; fr. خلص 'to be free from admixture,' &c.
def: s.m. The choice or best part of anything, the essence, extract, quintessence, flower, substance, gist, pith; sum; abstract, abridgment; conclusion, inference, moral; out-come (of work or action); — adj. Clear, free, unencumbered; loose; spacious, roomy, wide, open; plain, apparent, evident; — ḵẖulāṣa, ḵẖulāṣa-ḵẖulāṣa, adv. Giving the substance; expressly, explicitly; clearly, distinctly, plainly; openly, publicly
subs: (-kā), To make an abstract (of); to give the substance (of); &c. ( = iḵẖtis̤ār karnā, q.v.); — to make clear, to explain, to resolve
(-kā), To make an abstract (of), to summarise; to sum up.

def: s.f. = ḵẖulāṣa, q.v.
s.f. Replacing the old shoes on a horse after dressing his hoofs.

ety: inf. n. of خلد 'to remain or abide'
def: s.m. The abode of the state of perpetual existence; eternity; paradise; the world to come.
(v.n. fr. خلف), s.m.(?) Breach of promise.
s.m. (Dakkhinī) A buffalo, a bison.
s.m. Galangal, Alpinia galanga.
s.f. Nature, natural disposition or temper, &c.; quality; good disposition, affability, urbanity, politeness, civility, good manners.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَلْق (xalq), borrowed from Arabic خَلْق (ḵalq).

def: adv. Open; uncovered; — openly, publicly; freely, without restraint; at freedom or leisure; at ease (e.g. khulke baiṭhnā); — copiously; to (one's) satisfaction.
v.n. To do or act openly; — to indulge openly in vices which were formerly practised in secret.

ety: for A. خلة, inf. n. of iii of خلّ 'to pierce,' &c.
def: s.f. True, or sincere, friendship; friendship.
s.f. 'Opening and shutting'; a valve

ety: khul˚ = Prk. खुड्ड(इ) or खुल्ल(इ) = S. खुड्य(ते), rt. खुड्
def: v.n. To open, come open or undone; to open, expand, blow (as a flower; com. khilnā); to open out, unravel; to be opened (as a knot, or a road for traffic, &c.); to be disentangled, be unravelled; to be untied or unfastened; to be uncovered, be unfolded, be exposed, be laid bare; to be laid or cut open, be dissected, be analyzed; — to be expanded, be widened or enlarged; to be developed; — to be solved (as a problem or mystery), to be made known, be disclosed, be divulged, be revealed (to, -par); — to become clear, evident, or intelligible (to, -par); — to get loose; to be let loose, be set free (from restraint or control); to be loosed (as the tongue); to be dispersed (as clouds), to become clear, to clear up (the sky); to be dissipated or lost (as money at play); to be dissolved (as the marriage-bond); to be removed (as an obstacle or check); to be cleared (as a drain); to be broken (as meditation); to be laid aside (as restraint); — to begin, commence (as work); to start, leave (as a coach, or a railway train); to move (in chess); — to be opened, be started, be set up, be established (a shop, or a business, &c.); to be restored, be renewed (as withheld pay, &c.); to be recovered, to come back (as appetite); — to expand, dilate, or open kindly (upon); to be intimate or familiar (with, -par); to become favourable (the fates, or destiny); — to acquire fulness, clearness, brightness, or depth (a colour); to stand out well or conspicuously, to appear to advantage (with or on, -par), to go or match well (with, as one colour with another, &c.)
subs: v.n. intens. of khulnā (and the more common form).

def: v.n. To be inflamed with anger; to be angry, vexed, or displeased (cf. jhulasnā).

ety: for A. خلطة ḵẖult̤at, fem.; fr. خلط 'to mix'
def: s.m. Sociableness, familiarity, intimacy; association, partnership, copartnership.

ety: for خلوٌ ḵẖulwuṉ, inf. n. of خلو
def: s.m. Vacuity, &c. ( = ḵẖalā, q.v.).

ety: inf. n. of خلص 'to be pure'
def: s.m. Purity; sincerity, candour, integrity, friendship, affection.
v.t. To cause to be opened, &c. (see kholnā)
s.m. A large vessel or jar; an alembic, a still
subs: To boil clothes preparatory to washing them
ḵẖum-kada, s.m. A liquor-shop, a tavern.

def: s.m. (Dakh.) corr. of kumbh, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. خمر 'to cover'; see ḵẖamr, 'wine'
def: s.m. Intoxicating, intoxication; the effects of intoxication, pain and headache, &c. occasioned by drinking, crapulence, crop-sickness; headache or sickness (arising from want of sleep, &c.); languor; languishing appearance of the eyes (the effect of drinking, or of drowsiness, or of love, &c.); languishing look
subs: or ḵẖumār-ālūda, adj. Intoxicated; intoxicating (eyes); languishing (eyes)
s.f. The alleviating the effects of intoxication; anything used for that purpose, as tea, soda-water, &c.).

def: adj. & s.m. Given to drinking wine, drunken; — languishing; — one who is drunk; — s.f. = ḵẖumār, q.v.

ety: rel. n. fr. ḵẖumās, 'five together'; see ḵẖams
def: adj. & s.f. Consisting of or belonging to five; — a word composed of five radical (or radical and augmentative) letters; — a sort of magic pentagon.
s.f. A mushroom; a toadstool, a fungus.
v.n. To wax old.
s.m. A caste of Musalmans who weave mats; a member of that caste; a mat-weaver (see ḵẖumra).

ety: for A. خمرة ḵẖumrat, v.n. fr. خمر 'to cover,' &c.
def: s.m. A small pot or jar, a vessel for leaven; — a small drum (carried by beggars); — a small mat of palm leaves (for kneeling on at prayers); — a sect of faqīrs or Mohammadan mendicants.
part.

ety: Pehl. khun; Zend vohuni; S. vasā
def: s.m. Blood; killing, slaughter, murder
subs: ḵẖūn-āba, s.m. Bloody water; blood; a torrent of blood; tears of blood; blood turned to water (through grief)
s.f. Pouring out of blood, shedding of blood
(āṅkhoṅ-meṅ) utarnā, To be blood-shot (the eyes, — with rage, &c.)
adj. Shedding (or raining) blood (generally spoken of the eyes of a lover)
s.f. The raining or weeping of blood
The blood to become impure; to be leprous
adj. Bound or clotted together with blood
s.f. Clotting of blood; binding together or cementing with blood
s.m. The price of blood, blood-money, a fine for blood-shedding
v.n. To pass blood (as from hemorrhoids, &c.)
To suppress (one's) feelings, restrain (one's) emotion, or anger, or grief, &c.; — to consume (one's own) life-blood; to vex or worry (oneself) to death; — to work (oneself) to death
v.n. Blood to issue, to bleed
s.m. Bloodshed, bloody work, deeds of death; mutual slaughter
To do deeds of death, to make great havoc, &c.
adj. & s.m. Bloodthirsty, murderous, sanguinary, cruel; — a bloodthirsty man (or other animal), a murderer; an animal that preys on others
s.f. Bloodthirstiness; blood-shedding, slaughter, murder; — sorrow
adj. & s.m. Blood-shedding, murderous; — a blood-shedder, executioner, murderer, cut-throat, assassin
(-kā), To be distracted in consequence of having assassinated a person; — to be bent on taking the life or lives (of)
'To turn the blood white'; (fig.) to be unkind; to be displeased
To lack natural affection; to be unkind
adj. Thirsting for the blood (of, -kā), desirous of (one's) death
s.m. Compensation or fine for bloodshed, blood-wite; retaliation for blood-shedding, blood for blood
(-kā), To shed the blood (of), to kill, murder, assassinate; — to lavish, squander, waste
adj. 'Whose blood is taken,' doomed to be slain or to die, at the point of death
v.n. A murder to be committed; to be murdered; — to be wasted, be squandered.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خُون (xūn). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi خون (xvn /⁠xūn⁠/).

def: s.f. A murderess.

ety: prob. S. घृणस्, cf. ghūn; or S. हिंसा
def: s.m. & f. Aversion, dislike, loathing; animosity; spite, grudge, rancour, malignity; vindictiveness; envy.
v.n. = khunsānā, q.v.
s.f. The tendon Achilles
s.m. = ḵẖẉānca, q.v.
v.t. A dialec. form of khoṅćṇā, 'to prick.'
adj. (f. -ī), Blunted, blunt, turned (as the edge of a knife)
subs: (-ke), To shave the head (of a person) with a blunt razor; (met.) to torture, torment, worry

def: s.f. (Dakkhinī) Trampling under foot, crushing.

ety: fr. S. क्षुद्, with H caus. aff. al
def: v.t. To trample under foot, to tread upon, to crush; to beat or work with the feet.
s.m. = khuṅḍlā, q.v.

ety: prob. Prk. खुडालओ or खुडलओ; S. खण्ड+आल, or र+कः
def: s.m. A ruin, &c. ( = khaṅḍar, q.v.); — the hollow of a tree.

ety: khūṅd˚ = S. क्षुणत्(ति), rt. क्षुद्; — cf. khuṅdalnā
def: v.t. To dig up (as the earth with the feet); to work with the feet; to paw (as a horse); — to trample upon.
adj. & s.m. Relating to blood, or to murder; bloody, sanguinary; — a murderer, an assassin.
adj. Bloody; blood-coloured
= H خنثا ḵẖuns̤ā, s.m. corr. of next, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. خنث 'to fold or double,' &c.
def: s.m. A hermaphrodite; — asphodel.
= H خنث ḵẖuns̤, s.m. corr. of next, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = khunsī.

ety: khunsā˚ = khunas+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. aug.
def: v.n. To feel dislike or aversion (for); to be spiteful or vindictive, &c.; to be irritated or wrathful; to be cross.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Irritable, wrathful, splenetic, cross; — spiteful, vindictive, rancorous, malignant.

ety: prob. fr. Prk. खुटृ˚; see khūṭnā
def: s.m. Part, side, quarter, direction; station; corner, angle; pivot, central point; wheeling point (in military evolution); — portion, share; — ear-wax; — cake offered to a goddess; — adv. In the direction of, towards
subs: or ćau-khūṅṭ, s.m. The four corners or quarters of the world.

def: s.m. A stump; a stake (of wood), a pile; a tent-peg; a peg, pin; a pivot; the wooden handle of a quern or handmill; the upright post in a sugar-mill; — (met.) support, protection
subs: (-ko), 'To beat against a peg or stake;' to spurn, scorn
'To skip or jump to the extent of one's tether'; to presume on the protection of (another)
(-ko), 'To tie (one) to a butcher's stake or peg'; to expose (one) to danger; to put (one) in an unpleasant or trying situation; to confound; — to marry (a girl) to a cruel person.

def: adj. (f. -ī) = khoṅṭā = khoṭā, q.v.
s.f. A peg, pin, cog; short stubble; very short hair.
s.m. Name of a drug.
adj. Cold; cool, temperate; — happy, fortunate.
s.f. Coldness; coolness, temperateness; — prosperity.
s.m. Modulation, melody, singing, song
v.t. (Dakkhinī) To thrust in, to drive or force in, to stick, pierce, transfix, &c. ( = khubnā, q.v.).

ety: by redupl. of khup˚ = khub˚ or khubh˚ = S. क्षुभ्
def: v.n. To prick or pierce (into, -meṅ), to rankle (in); to feel envy, to envy; — to be agitated; to fall into a passion or rage.
s.f. (Dakkhinī) Envy, spite; rankling.
adj. & s.m. = خور ḵẖẉur or ḵẖur, q.v.
s.m. A cloven hoof; hoof (of a horse, &c.)
subs: s.m. Hoof-clattering, noise of the galloping of horses
adj. (f. -ī), Having a hoof or hoofs, hoofed.

def: s.f. Coughing (with a barking or rattling sound)
s.m. A furrow.
adj. (in comp.) Possessed of a hoof, hoofed; — s.m. The heel (of a shoe or sandal; — cf. khurī).

ety: prob. fr. S. क्षुरे+तक्ष; cf. kharoćnā, kharoṭnā, khureṅćnā
def: v.t. To scrape; to scratch; to scratch out, to erase
s.f. An instrumen for scraping or scratching, a scraper; an eraser; a scoop a shovel.

ety: for A. خرافة, fr. خرف 'to dote, to talk nonsense'
def: s.f. Fable, tale, romance; idle tale; ludicrous saying, folly, nonsense, improper or indecent speech.
s.f. pl. (of ḵẖurāfat), Fables, &c.; — (as a sing.) senseless or improper speech, folly; obscene language, smut
subs: To talk nonsense; to use improper or indecent language.

def: v.n. (dialec.) = khaṛuknā, q.v.
s.m. The crust which forms on a healing wound or sore, cicatrice, scab, scale (syn. khūṅṭ or khūṭhī, q.v.; — dialec. forms are khuranṭ, khuṅṭar).
adj. & s.m. See کهرانٿ khurāṅṭ.

ety: prob. S. क्षुर + आवृत्तः? 'having the hoofs turned or twisted'
def: adj. Very old; — experienced, knowing; crafty, cunning; — s.m. Old soil, soil trodden down by the hoofs of cattle.
s.m. (Dakkhinī) A fowl-house, a hen-coop; — an aviary.
adj. Of or belonging to the country of Ḵẖurāsān (the ancient Bactriana), native of ḵẖurāsān; — ḵẖurāsānī ajwān, s.f. Seeds of Hyoscyamus niger, black henbane.

ety: for A. خراشة ḵẖurāshat, fem.; fr. خرش 'to scratch'
def: s.m. Scale or filing (of iron); — a small debt.
s.f. corr. of khul-bandī, q.v.
s.f. Scraping; scrapings (of a pot, &c.); — a kind of sweetmeat made of scrapings from a milk-pot.
adj. Small, little, minute; young
subs: adj. Of tender years, young; under age
s m. The apartments of the concubines of great men.

def: s.m. Anything small, a small qnantity, a bit, fragment, fraction, a particle; a crumb; a speck; a slight blemish; a pedlar's small or trifling wares; small coin, small change
subs: v.t. To sell in small quantities or in retail
adj. & s.m. Acute; hypercritical; — a critic, a caviller
s.f. Subtilty of genius, acuteness; hypercriticism
To seize on trifling faults, to cavil, to criticise
s.m. A vender of small wares, a pedlar, a huckster
v.t. To reduce to small pieces; — to change (money); — to sell, dispose of
adj. & s.m. Fault-finding, fault-seeking; cavilling; — a caviller, a critic
s.f. Seizing on trifling faults, cavilling

def: adj. (f. -ī), Cut up, broken up; rough, rugged; furrowed, wrinkled.
s.m. Roughness, ruggedness, &c.
s.f. (fr. ḵẖurd), Smallness, littleness; childhood, infancy; minority.

ety: ḵẖurd = ḵẖurda+iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: s.m. A money-changer.

ety: ḵẖurd = ḵẖurda+iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: s.m. A money-changer.
s.f. = kharoṅṭ, q.v.
s.f. A scratch, &c. ( = khureṅć = kharoć, q.v.); — a rankling; enmity; spite, rancour, malice
subs: (apnī), To vent (one's) spite (on), &c.; — to find fault (with), carp or cavil (at).

def: adj. & s.m. Spiteful, vindictive, malicious; — censorious, carping; — a spiteful or vindictive person; — a carper, caviller.
v.t. = kharoṭnā, or kharoćnā, q v.

ety: a dialec. form of khadeṛnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To run after, to chase; to persecute; to catch; to enclose.
s.m. Purslain, Portulaca oleracea.

ety: for A. خرفة ḵẖurfat, fem.; fr. خرف 'to gather or pluck'
def: s.m. That which is plucked from a tree, gathered or plucked fruits; autumnal fruit.
s.f. A cloven hoof, &c. ( = khur, q.v.); — heel (of a shoe); a piece of leather put on the heel of a shoe (by which it is pulled up).
s.f. An instrument like a cup (formed of a cocoanut-shell, or other material) with which clothes are crimped or marked with stripes.
s.f. The knee-pan ( = khurpā).

ety: prob. fr. A. ḵẖurj, rt. خرج, through P. ḵẖurjīn; but said to be fr. ḵẖẉur-ćīn
def: s.f. A saddle-bag; a sack, a wallet; a gunny bag for holding tents or other goods (loaded on camels, or other beasts of burden).
adj. (f. -ī), Rough, rugged, uneven.
adj. (f. -ī), Having holes or gaps, rugged, uneven, rough; — s.m. A hole or hollow (in the ground, or in a tree, &c.); a fowl-house; a roost.

ety: Prk. खड = S. खण्ड+dim. aff. ली = S. ल+इका
def: s.f. A stall or manger (in a stable).
